channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Black Ops 7 Director Addresses Skill-Based Damage Accusations
item.content:encoded:
Skill-based matchmaking conversations have dominated the majority of Black Ops 7, but a recent trend has seen the community talking about a different type of gameplay influence, namely skill-based damage differences.

Call of Duty: Black Ops 7 launched with open matchmaking, AKA no SBMM. Meaning, your lobbies are not determined by stats and player ability (for the most part). However, SBMM has factored into the game more as time has gone on, and players are now accusing Black Ops 7 of having skill-based damage. A Treyarch dev has shut down these suggestions.

Skill-Based Damage Not Used in Black Ops 7

Treyarch Design Director Matt Scronce has responded to Reidboyy’s post that ‘skill-based damage is 100% real and in the game.’

Scronce had this to say about the topic:

“I can promise you, there’s nothing behind the scenes modifying any of our damage values.”

So, if you feel you’re getting different damage output compared to other people in your lobby, this is merely a figment of your imagination. Your lobby might determine who you’re put against, but the only thing affecting your damage is potentially ping or netcode.

It came about because of a Pirat_Nation post mentioning Sony’s patent that dynamically affects gameplay for different players on the fly. In theory, this WOULD be skill-based damage, and could help players floundering in a match, or give someone dominating a lobby a roadblock.

You can check out the full exchange right here.

SBMM is one thing, but how does skill-based damage sound to you? If it’s implemented across different multiplayer games, including CoD, would it stop you from partaking in online shooters or multiplayer in general? Let us know through the Insider Gaming Discord.

Check out the full Black Ops 7 & Warzone Season 3 Release Date & Start Times for more CoD content.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Second PS Plus Monthly Game for April Revealed
item.content:encoded:
Another addition to PlayStation Plus Monthly Games for April 2026 has been revealed, and this one will be enjoyed by anime fans: Sword Art Online: Fractured Daydream.

A report from last week revealed that the PlayStation Plus Monthly Games headliner for April 2026 will be Lords of the Fallen. Now, the same insider has also provided us with the second addition for this month, along with other details, including when the games will be available to download for PS Plus Essential members in April and more.

PlayStation Plus April 2026 Monthly Games Will Introduce Sword Art Online: Fractured Daydream

The latest report arrives from the reliable billbil-kun through Dealabs. It confirms that the second title for PlayStation Plus free monthly games in April is Sword Art Online: Fractured Daydream. It wasn’t confirmed whether a third game will be revealed as well. The report also states that the official announcement from PlayStation should arrive on April 1, and the game should be available to download from April 7.

Sword Art Online: Fractured Daydream is a new adventure where 21 characters from the SAO series join Kirito in a new battle. The title has a single-player story mod featuring an original story in which “Characters from across time and space, each with their own destiny, gather to confront the anomaly in the VR world.” There are also online modes, such as boss raids, co-op quests, and free roam.

In boss raids, players will see 20 players in five parties of four players each, take down gigantic bosses. Co-op quest has five parties of four starting together to gradually meet in a final showdown against a boss as the quest progresses. Free roam allows players to freely explore a vast map filled with powerful bosses and treasure chests.

In other news, the prices of PlayStation 5, PS5 Pro, and PlayStation Portal devices are about to increase significantly worldwide. Additionally, since the PS5 launched in 2020, PlayStation has shuttered eight of its studios, and they’re all listed here. What are your thoughts on the second addition to PlayStation Plus monthly games for April? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Fortnite teasing the return of one of the all-time greatest collabs. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

item.title: Fortnite Teases Return of One of The All-Time Greatest Collabs
item.content:encoded:
Fortnite players have plenty to digest with the recent switch to a new season, but the slew of content is not slowing down, and one of the greatest collabs of all time is poised to return.

Epic Games has become renowned for huge collabs bringing a host of different franchises into the game, and while this has become a core part of the Fortnite model, it wasn’t always that way.

In fact, there is one event in particular that can be seen as a turning point, and Fortnite is teasing its return.

Fortnite Avengers Collab Returning to OG

Fortnite OG Season 8 launches on April 1, rolling back the years to bring Chapter 1, Season 8, into the limelight once again, and a popular limited-time mode has been teased for a return.

Way back in 2019, Fortnite had a huge Avengers: Endgame crossover event in Season 8, with a game mode that split players into two teams: Avengers and Chitauri. Now, Fortnite has teased its return.

In a post on social media, with the target date of April 3, Fortnite’s official Twitter account posted an image of Brite Bomber holding Captain America’s shield, while the post had the tagline of ‘Whatever it takes’.

This confirms that the popular Endgame LTM mode is poised to return this week, rolling back the years to arguably Fortnite’s greatest-ever collab.

In the LTM, the Avengers team had to eliminate 150 Chitauri enemies to disable their respawn ability, before then wiping them out, and Thanos, while the Chitauri goal was to collect all the Infinity Stones, and then eliminate all the Avengers enemies.

By collecting Infinity Stones, players on the Chitauri team transformed into Thanos, who was equipped with a punch ability, a beam attack, and another attack to slam into the ground from the air.

Both teams also had special weapons at their disposal, including Captain America’s Shield, Hawkeye’s Bow, Iron Man’s Repulsors, Thor’s Stormbreaker, the Chitauri Laser Rifle, and the Chitauri Energy Launcher.

Fortnite’s Avengers: Endgame collab and LTM paved the way for other huge collabs to follow, which have now become a mainstay in the game, and rolling it back this week is sure to attract heaps of players.

Are you excited for the Avengers LTM to return to Fortnite? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Fortnite, check out all Chapter 7 Season 2 Week 2 quests, and take a look at the news of the closure of several game modes.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

item.title: NYT Mini Crossword Hints Today—Answers For Friday, March 30
item.content:encoded:
The NYT Mini Crossword is a popular, quick-to-complete word game to test yourself daily, but some of the puzzles can be difficult to complete, so here are some hints.

If you’ve found yourself stuck on today’s NYT Mini, Insider Gaming has some helpful hints and all the answers for the NYT Mini Crossword on Monday, March 30, 2026.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s edition of the NYT Mini Crossword. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How to Play NYT Mini Crossword

The NYT Mini Crossword is available to play on the New York Times Games app or website, but, as of September 2025, a New York Times Game subscription is required.

The NYT Games Subscription is priced at $6.95 per month, but there are introductory offers available for other NYT subscriptions that include games access, which might be worth considering.

Without a Games subscription, access to the NYT Mini Crossword is blocked.

NYT Mini Crossword Clues (March 30)

Here are all the clues provided for the NYT Mini Crossword for March 30, 2026.

Across

1 -1975 Spielberg film that’s considered the first summer blockbuster

5 – “Oh okay, gotcha”

6 – Athlete from New York (in one sport) or San Francisco (in another)

8 – Declare publicly

9 – Emperor who didn’t actually fiddle while Rome burned

Down

1 – “The ___ is up!”

2 – Like lotus root and wax apples

3 – Drive dangerously in traffic

4 – Spanish title

7 – Scrabble value of D or G

Additional Clues For NYT Mini (March 30)

Here are some additional clues to help you solve the NYT Mini Crossword on March 30, 2026.

Across

1 – Blockbuster film about a man-eating shark

5 – Aha

6 – All-time wrestling great, Andre the ____

8 – Declaration made at a wedding

9 – Final emperor of the Julio-Claudian dynasty

Down

1 – Like a dance

2 – Like a K-Pop singer

3 – Like knitting and sewing

4 – ‘Mr’ in Spanish

7 – Second number

All NYT Mini Crossword Answers (March 30)

Here are all the answers for the NYT Mini Crossword on March 30, 2026.

Across

1 – JAWS

5 – I SEE

6 – GIANT

8 – AVOW

9 – NERO

Down

1 – JIG

2 – ASIAN

3 – WEAVE

4 – SENOR

7 – TWO

When is The Next Game?

The NYT Mini Crossword refreshes daily at 10 PM ET on weekdays and Saturdays, and at 6 PM ET on Sundays.

As such, the next NYT Mini Crossword will be available at 7 PM PT/9 PM CT/10 PM ET/3 AM GMT/2 PM AET.

What is The NYT Mini Crossword?

The NYT Mini Crossword was introduced in 2014 as a shorter and quicker-to-complete game to accompany the main New York Times Crossword, which was initially released in 1942.

Until September 2025, the NYT Mini Crossword was free, but it has now been consolidated into a monthly NYT Games Subscription alongside several other popular games.

Did you struggle with the NYT Mini Crossword today? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Today’s Contexto: Hints & Answer For March 30, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Contexto is a word association game that gives you a new word to guess every single day. With every attempt, the AI algorithm determines how close your guess is to the daily answer based on thematic and contextual similarity.

Sometimes, though, no matter how experienced you are, the word you’re looking for can be incredibly vague and hard to deduce. Luckily for you, this guide is full of hints to give you a helping hand, but make sure you give today’s Contexto a go if you haven’t already!

How To Play Contexto

Contexto is incredibly simple to play, but difficult to master. All you have to do is type words into the search bar, and the AI algorithm will tell you how close your word is to the answer.

You’ll know how close your guess is based on the colour of the bar and the number next to it.

  • Red includes guesses with the number 1,501 and above, meaning you’re way off from the secret word.
  • Orange includes guesses between 301 and 1,500, meaning you’re getting closer, but still some ways off.
  • Green includes guesses between 1 and 300, meaning you’re on the right track.

Best Contexto Tips & Tricks

  • Start off using broad terms, then narrow down once you’ve got under the 1,000 mark.
  • Try using Nouns first, and dip into verbs or adjectives once you’ve discovered the context.
  • Experiment with random words at times, especially as different forms of a root word can rank differently, such as work and worker.
  • Guess away, as unlike other word games like Wordle, you aren’t limited to a strict number of attempts.

Best Starting Words For Contexto

Here are some great starting words to get you going with Contexto:

  • Thing
  • Person
  • Place
  • Animal
  • Work
  • Nature
  • Game
  • Concept
  • Idea
  • Food

Contexto Hints For Today, March 30, 2026

Without giving away today’s Contexto answer, here are a few helpful hints to get you thinking in the right direction:

  • Hint 1: Needed for a balanced diet
  • Hint 2: Thin thread
  • Hint 3: Optic cables

If they don’t help, then here are the nine closest words, going from tenth to second:

  • Fabric
  • Fiberglass
  • Multimode
  • Cable
  • Composite
  • Ethernet
  • Carbon
  • Optical
  • Optic

Today’s Contexto Answer For March 30, 2026

If you’ve tried everything and still can’t figure out today’s Contexto answer, then you can find it down below:

Answer: Fiber

Fiber is a thin, strong structure that forms plants, muscles, and artificial materials. They’re not digested and pass through the body as waste.

When Is The Next Contexto Game?

Contexto is updated daily at 12 AM or Midnight in your respective time zone, meaning the next game will start on March 31.

The Contexto schedule is very consistent, with a new game releasing at the same time every day.

How did you find today’s Contexto? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more word game guides, check out the following:

  • Wordle

MOBILE APP
